_comment: TRUE POSITIVE — SP-105002: PsExec lateral movement to Domain Controller (T1021.002)
scenario: Attacker uses PsExec (or similar) to execute commands on the Domain Controller after compromising a workstation
why_tp: PSEXESVC.exe written to ADMIN$ share on Domain Controller — textbook PsExec pattern
New service PSEXESVC installed on DC (Event 7045) — confirms remote execution
Type 3 (Network) logon from workstation 10.10.5.42 to DC01 at 22:01 (after hours)
jdoe is a regular user account, not a domain admin — suspicious privilege use
analyst_action: IMMEDIATE: Check if jdoe has legitimate domain admin rights (should not for a regular user)
Review all commands executed via PSEXESVC on DC01 between 22:01 and service removal
Check if DC01 was used to execute further commands (e.g., net user /add, DCSync)
Review how jdoe obtained domain admin credentials (likely from prior LSASS dump)
